response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r1c4 is in critical distress: islanded operation with SoC at 33% (0.99 kWh of 3 kWh capacity) and 1.0 kW load means depletion in ~1 hour without intervention. All recent outbound sharing requests have been rejected by neighbors (r0c4, r1c3, r2c4) citing their own critical SoC levels (44–49%) and systemic microgrid scarcity. The pattern is clear: a cascading depletion spiral where no peer has surplus to share. With no established trust circles and no grid reconnection signal, r1c4 must immediately shift to pure survival mode—cease all outbound sharing attempts, enforce an aggressive SoC floor to preserve load-serving capacity, and signal urgent inbound need to any peer still capable of response. The microgrid-wide scarcity belief is validated by unanimous rejections; further sharing attempts waste battery cycles and delay critical load shedding decisions.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Microgrid-wide cascading depletion confirmed. All peers (r0c4, r1c3, r2c4) at critical SoC 44–49% rejecting outbound. r1c4 SoC 33% with 1.0 kW load = ~60 min to depletion. Islanded, no trust circles. Suspend all sharing; prioritize own survival and load shedding readiness. Inbound requests only."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
